title: Thomson Foundation Young Journalist Award 2026: Global Competition for Emerging Journalists Under 30
summary: The Thomson Foundation Young Journalist Award 2026 is now accepting applications from talented young journalists worldwide. Recognized as one of the most prestigious international journalism competitions, the award celebrates outstanding reporting by early-career journalists who demonstrate courage, originality, accountability, and a commitment to public interest journalism. Since its launch 14 years ago, the Young Journalist Award has become a globally respected platform that recognizes exceptional reporting from journalists working in some of the world s most challenging environments. The competition has received nearly 4,000 entries and has highlighted remarkable journalism from countries such as Kenya, Afghanistan, India, Lebanon, Malaysia, Peru, Sudan , and many others. Winning or becoming a finalist is more than receiving an award—it is an opportunity to showcase your journalism on the international stage, connect with leading editors and reporters, strengthen your professional network, and access career-defining opportunities. If you are an emerging journalist under the age of 30 and reporting from an eligible country, this competition could become a major milestone in your journalism career. About the Thomson Foundation Young Journalist Award The Thomson Foundation Young Journalist Award recognizes exceptional young journalists whose work contributes to public interest journalism and promotes accountability, transparency, and social change. The competition seeks reporting that: Exposes corruption or wrongdoing. Holds institutions accountable. Explains complex issues clearly. Amplifies underrepresented voices. Investigates issues affecting communities. Demonstrates originality and courage. Creates measurable public impact. The award welcomes journalists working across different media platforms and storytelling formats. Why the Young Journalist Award Matters Journalism plays a critical role in strengthening democracy, informing the public, and ensuring accountability. However, young journalists often face limited opportunities for international recognition despite producing impactful reporting. The Thomson Foundation created this award to provide talented journalists with: International recognition Career development opportunities Professional networking Exposure to global media organizations Access to experienced journalists and editors Specialized journalism training For many previous finalists and winners, the award has opened doors to international reporting opportunities, collaborations, fellowships, and leadership positions within global media organizations. What Winners and Finalists Receive Three outstanding finalists will be selected to travel to London, United Kingdom , where they will participate in several prestigious professional activities. Finalists will receive the opportunity to: Attend the Foreign Press Association Media Awards The three finalists will attend the Foreign Press Association Media Awards on 30 November 2026 , where the overall winner will be officially announced before an audience of distinguished journalists, editors, and international media professionals. Visit Leading UK Newsrooms While in London, finalists will tour major news organizations, giving them valuable exposure to international newsroom operations and editorial practices. Participate in Specialized Journalism Training Finalists will also take part in a customized professional development workshop designed specifically to strengthen their: Investigative reporting skills Storytelling techniques Ethical journalism practices Professional networking abilities Career development strategies Career Benefits of the Award Receiving recognition through the Young Journalist Award can significantly enhance a journalist s career. Benefits include: International exposure Recognition from leading journalism experts Expanded professional network Increased credibility Opportunities for future collaborations Greater visibility among international media organizations Enhanced career prospects Many previous finalists have reported that the award helped them secure new professional opportunities and collaborations. Success Stories from Previous Winners The Young Journalist Award has helped launch the careers of numerous outstanding journalists. Yousra Elbagir The 2016 Young Journalist Award winner , Yousra Elbagir, is now the Africa Correspondent for Sky News , demonstrating how the award can become a stepping stone toward an internationally recognized journalism career. Tracy Bonareri Onchoke The 2025 winner , Tracy Bonareri Onchoke from Kenya, received praise from the judging panel for her: Rigorous reporting Persistence Commitment to accountability journalism Her award-winning portfolio investigated why hundreds of Kenyan law graduates were failing the national bar examination. Following her victory, Tracy explained that winning the award expanded her professional opportunities by helping her connect with journalists worldwide and collaborate on meaningful reporting projects. Her experience demonstrates how international recognition can accelerate both personal and professional growth. What Kind of Journalism is Eligible? The Thomson Foundation welcomes a broad range of journalism formats. Applicants may submit work in: Print journalism Broadcast journalism Audio journalism Video journalism Multimedia journalism Data journalism Visual journalism Investigative journalism Human-interest reporting Public interest journalism Regardless of format, judges are looking for work that demonstrates: Originality Courage Accuracy Strong storytelling Public impact Accountability Professional excellence Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Thomson Foundation Young Journalist Award 2026, applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Age Requirement Applicants must: Be 30 years old or younger on 30 November 2026 . Country Eligibility Applicants must: Be from or reporting in a country whose Gross National Income (GNI) per capita is USD 20,000 or less . Journalism Portfolio Applicants must submit: Three published or broadcast pieces of journalism. The work: Must have been produced during the 12 months before 9 August 2026 . May be submitted in: Print Audio Video Multimedia formats Verification Letter Applicants must also provide a verification letter confirming authorship of the submitted work. The letter should be: Written on official letterhead. Signed by the applicant s editor. Freelance journalists may instead submit: A letter from a commissioning editor, or A journalism academic familiar with their work. Why You Should Apply The Young Journalist Award is an opportunity to gain international recognition for journalism that makes a difference. Whether you investigate corruption, report on climate change, document conflict, highlight human rights issues, or tell underreported community stories, the competition values journalism that creates positive impact. Applicants have the chance to: Showcase their work internationally. Strengthen professional credibility. Connect with respected journalists worldwide. Learn from leading media professionals. Build long-term international networks. Advance their journalism careers. How to Apply Interested applicants should: Confirm they meet all eligibility requirements. Select three of their strongest published or broadcast journalism pieces. Obtain a verification letter from their editor or eligible referee. Complete the official application before the deadline. Applicants are encouraged to review all submission requirements carefully before applying HERE Learn more about the opportunity HERE Application Deadline 9th August 2026 Late applications are unlikely to be considered, so eligible journalists are encouraged to prepare their materials well in advance. Conclusion The Thomson Foundation Young Journalist Award 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for emerging journalists to gain global recognition, strengthen their reporting skills, and expand their professional networks. By celebrating journalism that informs, challenges, and creates positive social change, the award continues to elevate talented reporters from around the world. Whether you are an investigative reporter uncovering corruption, a multimedia storyteller documenting community issues, or a broadcaster giving voice to marginalized populations, this competition provides a platform to showcase your work before an international audience of leading journalism professionals. If you meet the eligibility requirements, this is your opportunity to take your journalism career to the next level. For more Global COMPETITION updates click HERE The post Thomson Foundation Young Journalist Award 2026: Global Competition for Emerging Journalists Under 30 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully-Funded Spring 2027 Ph.D. Fellowship: EcoClimate AI Lab, University of Houston
summary: The EcoClimate AI Lab at the Department of Earth and Atmospheric Sciences, University of Houston, is inviting applications for a fully funded Ph.D. fellowship beginning in Spring 2027 . This fellowship program is tailored for students who are passionate about applying artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) to address urgent challenges in environmental and Earth system sciences . With a strong emphasis on interdisciplinary collaboration, this opportunity provides students with access to world-class resources, mentorship, and research networks that will prepare them to become leaders in climate and ecosystem science. About the EcoClimate AI Lab The EcoClimate AI Lab is a pioneering research group dedicated to advancing our understanding of how terrestrial and coastal ecosystems respond to climate dynamics and disturbances. These disturbances include: Wildfires – analyzing how fire events disrupt carbon cycles, vegetation recovery, and long-term ecosystem resilience. Flooding – studying hydrological impacts on soil, vegetation, and coastal systems, with a focus on resilience and adaptation. Drought – investigating water scarcity effects on land-atmosphere interactions and ecosystem productivity. Land-use and land-cover change – examining human-driven transformations and their ecological consequences. By integrating carbon, water, energy, and nutrient cycles , the lab develops predictive models that enhance our understanding of ecosystem-climate-human interactions . The ultimate goal is to advance nature-based solutions and mitigate natural hazards and climate risks . Collaborative Network Students in the EcoClimate AI Lab will benefit from collaborations with leading institutions, including: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory Stanford University University of Texas at Austin This collaborative environment ensures exposure to diverse perspectives, advanced methodologies, and opportunities for joint projects that broaden academic and professional horizons. The collaborative network is not limited to these institutions. Students may also engage with government agencies, NGOs, and international research consortia. This exposure ensures that graduates are prepared to work in diverse environments, from academia to policy-making. The lab’s partnerships often lead to joint publications, shared datasets, and opportunities to attend specialized workshops or conferences. Position Details Ph.D. Student (Spring 2027) This fully funded position covers tuition and living expenses, enabling students to dedicate themselves fully to research without financial burden. Preferred Qualifications Applicants should demonstrate: 1. Technical Skills Proficiency in Python programming and ML/AI methods. Experience applying ML/AI to remote sensing, geospatial, or environmental data is highly desirable. Python is the backbone of modern scientific computing. Applicants should be comfortable with libraries such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, and Pandas. Knowledge of geospatial libraries like GDAL or rasterio is an added advantage. 2. Research Interests AI applications in environmental sciences. Remote sensing and Earth system modeling. Land-atmosphere interactions. Extreme events modeling. Coastal resilience studies. 3. Personal Attributes Strong intellectual curiosity. Self-motivation for independent scientific research. 4. Admission Requirements Applicants must meet University of Houston Graduate School admission requirements . International applicants must provide TOEFL/IELTS scores . Application Process Applicants should prepare a single PDF file named: “[Your Name] – PhD Application 2027” This file must include: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Academic Transcript Cover Letter (1–2 pages) outlining research experience, skills, and future interests Contact information for three references TOEFL/IELTS transcript (required for international applicants) Each document plays a critical role. The CV demonstrates your academic and professional trajectory. The transcript provides evidence of your academic preparation. The cover letter is your chance to connect your skills and interests with the lab’s mission. References validate your capabilities and character. Submission Details Email applications to: Dr. Kunxiaojia Yuan at kyuan@central.uh.edu Deadline Applications received by August 21, 2026, will receive full consideration. Review will continue until the position is filled. Early submission is strongly encouraged. Due to the high volume of applications, only shortlisted candidates may be contacted for further consideration. Applicants should avoid common mistakes such as submitting incomplete files, failing to name the PDF correctly, or writing generic cover letters. Tailoring your application to the EcoClimate AI Lab’s focus areas significantly increases your chances. Additional Opportunities The EcoClimate AI Lab is continuously recruiting for: Postdoctoral researchers Ph.D. and M.S. students Undergraduate research assistants Visiting students and scholars Interested individuals should reach out to Dr. Kunxiaojia Yuan with their CV and a brief introduction of their background and research interests. Postdoctoral researchers often lead independent projects while mentoring graduate students. M.S. students may focus on applied aspects of AI in environmental monitoring. Undergraduate assistants gain hands-on experience with data collection and analysis. Visiting scholars benefit from UH’s resources and contribute to the lab’s international outlook. About the University of Houston The Department of Earth Atmospheric Sciences at UH is one of the largest and best-funded departments in the U.S., situated within a top-tier R1 research institution . Students benefit from: Outstanding research resources Internship opportunities Professional networking connections Strong job placement records Life in Houston Houston, the fourth-largest city in the United States , offers: A vibrant, diverse metropolitan environment World-class museums and arts Championship sports teams An exceptional food scene Affordable living compared to other major U.S. cities This makes Houston an excellent place to live, study, and grow professionally. Houston is also a hub for industries such as energy, healthcare, and aerospace. Students benefit from proximity to NASA’s Johnson Space Center and the Texas Medical Center. The city’s affordability makes it easier for students to live comfortably while pursuing advanced degrees. Why Choose This Ph.D. Program? Fully Funded Support – Students receive financial coverage for tuition and living expenses. Cutting-Edge Research – Work at the intersection of AI and environmental sciences. Global Collaboration – Engage with leading institutions and experts worldwide. Career Advancement – Benefit from UH’s strong job placement record. Quality of Life – Live in one of the most affordable and diverse major U.S. cities. Graduates of this program often pursue careers in academia, government agencies, NGOs, and private companies specializing in sustainability and climate solutions. The program’s alumni network provides mentorship and career guidance long after graduation. To learn more about this program, visit HERE . Expanded Insights for Applicants To reach a deeper understanding of what this program entails, let’s explore additional aspects that make this Ph.D. opportunity unique: 1. Interdisciplinary Training Students will not only gain expertise in AI and ML but also acquire a strong foundation in environmental science, climate modeling, and geospatial analysis. This interdisciplinary training prepares graduates for careers in academia, government, and industry. 2. Research Impact The lab’s work contributes directly to global efforts to combat climate change, improve ecosystem resilience, and develop sustainable solutions. Students will have the chance to publish in high-impact journals and present at international conferences. 3. Mentorship and Guidance Faculty members provide personalized mentorship, guiding students through the complexities of research design, data analysis, and scientific communication. 4. Professional Development Beyond research, students will have access to workshops, seminars, and networking events that enhance professional skills such as grant writing, project management, and public engagement. Students will receive interdisciplinary training that includes GIS workshops, AI hackathons, and climate modeling seminars. Professional development opportunities include grant writing workshops, teaching assistantships, and science communication training. Students are also encouraged to engage in community outreach, sharing their research with local schools and organizations. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. Is this Ph.D. program fully funded? Yes, the program provides full funding, covering tuition and living expenses. 2. What is the application deadline? Applications received by August 21, 2026 will receive full consideration. Review will continue until the position is filled. 3. Who is eligible to apply? Applicants worldwide are eligible, provided they meet UH Graduate School requirements. International applicants must submit TOEFL/IELTS scores. 4. What programming skills are required? Proficiency in Python is essential. Experience with ML/AI applied to environmental or geospatial data is highly desirable. 5. Can I apply if my background is not in environmental sciences? Yes, applicants with strong AI/ML skills and interest in environmental applications are encouraged to apply, even if their prior background is in computer science or related fields. 6. What kind of research projects will I work on? Projects may involve modeling ecosystem responses to climate disturbances, analyzing remote sensing data, and developing AI-driven predictive models. 7. Are there opportunities for collaboration? Yes, students will collaborate with institutions such as Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Stanford University, and UT Austin. 8. What is life like in Houston? Houston is a diverse, affordable, and vibrant city with world-class cultural, sports, and food scenes. 9. How competitive is the program? The program is highly competitive due to its funding and interdisciplinary focus. Early submission is recommended. 10. What other opportunities are available in the lab? The lab also recruits postdoctoral researchers, M.S. students, undergraduate assistants, and visiting scholars. 11. Will I have opportunities to publish my research? Yes, students are encouraged to publish in peer-reviewed journals and present at international conferences. 12. What career paths can graduates pursue? Graduates can pursue careers in academia, government agencies, environmental consulting, and technology companies focusing on sustainability. 13. Do I need prior publications to apply? No, prior publications are not required, but research experience is highly valued. 14. Is GRE required? Check UH Graduate School requirements; Conclusion The fully funded Ph.D. position at the EcoClimate AI Lab, University of Houston , is a rare and valuable opportunity for students passionate about combining AI and environmental sciences . With strong institutional support, interdisciplinary collaboration, and a thriving city environment, this program is designed to nurture the next generation of scientific leaders in climate and ecosystem research. title: Cambridge Trust Postgraduate Scholarships 2026–2027 at the University of Cambridge: Fully Funded Master’s and PhD Opportunities for Outstanding Students Worldwide
summary: The University of Cambridge is one of the world s most prestigious academic institutions, attracting exceptional students from every corner of the globe. To ensure that talented individuals are not prevented from studying because of financial barriers, the Cambridge Trust offers a wide range of postgraduate scholarships that support outstanding students pursuing Master s and PhD degrees at Cambridge. The Cambridge Trust scholarships are among the University s flagship funding programmes, helping academically gifted students from both the UK and overseas undertake postgraduate study across nearly every academic discipline offered by the University. Whether you are planning to pursue groundbreaking research, advance your professional expertise, or prepare for an academic career, the Cambridge Trust provides financial support designed to help exceptional scholars achieve their educational ambitions. This comprehensive guide explains everything prospective applicants need to know, including eligibility requirements, scholarship benefits, application procedures, funding deadlines, selection criteria, and important application tips. What Are the Cambridge Trust Postgraduate Scholarships? The Cambridge Trust is a major provider of postgraduate funding at the University of Cambridge. It works together with numerous international governments, charities, foundations, corporations, and philanthropic partners to fund scholarships for outstanding students. Rather than offering a single scholarship, the Trust administers numerous scholarship programmes , each with its own funding arrangements and eligibility requirements. The scholarships are intended to attract the world s brightest students while ensuring that financial circumstances do not prevent deserving applicants from studying at Cambridge. Funding opportunities are available for students beginning postgraduate studies in a wide variety of academic disciplines. Scholarship Objectives The Cambridge Trust seeks to: Support academically exceptional postgraduate students. Remove financial barriers to higher education. Encourage international academic excellence. Promote diversity and equality across postgraduate education. Foster future leaders, researchers, innovators, and professionals. Available Degree Levels The Cambridge Trust provides funding for eligible students commencing: Master s Degrees Supported Master s programmes include (subject to eligibility): MPhil MASt (Master of Advanced Study) LLM (with certain restrictions) Other eligible Master s programmes offered by the University Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) Applicants intending to begin a PhD programme at Cambridge are eligible to apply. See other important information on eligibility here . Courses Not Supported The Cambridge Trust does not provide funding for: MBA programmes Master of Finance (MFin) Premium-level professional courses Part-time MSt (Master of Studies) programmes Eligible Fields of Study One of the greatest advantages of Cambridge Trust scholarships is that they support students from virtually every academic discipline offered at the University. Eligible subjects include areas such as: Engineering Medicine Law Business-related disciplines (excluding MBA and MFin) Natural Sciences Mathematics Computer Science Social Sciences Arts Humanities Education Environmental Sciences Public Health Economics Architecture Languages Psychology History Politics Philosophy Applicants should consult the University of Cambridge postgraduate course directory to confirm that their chosen programme is eligible. Cambridge Trust Scholarship Eligibility To be considered for funding, applicants generally need to demonstrate two major qualities: 1. Academic Excellence Applicants should possess: Outstanding academic achievements Strong educational background Excellent university grades High research potential Evidence of leadership within their academic discipline The Cambridge Trust places significant emphasis on academic merit when evaluating applications. 2. Financial Need Many Cambridge Trust scholarships require applicants to demonstrate genuine financial need. Applicants should be prepared to provide accurate information regarding income sources, including: Salary Business income Rental income Investments Dividends Other significant financial resources Not every scholarship requires financial need, as some awards are based solely on academic excellence. Nationality Requirements The Cambridge Trust supports both: Home (UK) students Overseas (international) students Many scholarship programmes are open globally. However, some scholarships are funded by external partners and may be limited to applicants from specific countries or regions. Applicants should carefully review the eligibility criteria for each scholarship before applying. Read more on fee status for postgraduates Equality and Diversity The Cambridge Trust is committed to providing equal opportunities to all applicants. Selection decisions are made based on the published scholarship criteria rather than personal characteristics such as: Sex Gender identity Marital status Parenthood Race Nationality Ethnicity Disability Sexual orientation Religion Age Important Eligibility Restrictions Applicants should note the following important rules: Funding is generally unavailable for students pursuing a qualification at the same or lower academic level than one already obtained. An exception exists for applicants undertaking a second MPhil as a departmental requirement before progressing to a PhD. Students already enrolled at Cambridge are generally not eligible unless they are progressing to a higher degree after graduation. Most scholarships support full-time study. Some part-time postgraduate courses may be considered where available. Part-time MSt programmes are not eligible. MSt is not the same as MASt (Master of Advanced Study) courses, which we do support. How to Apply for Cambridge Trust Scholarships Unlike many scholarship programmes, applicants do not submit a separate scholarship application directly to the Cambridge Trust for most awards. Instead, scholarship consideration forms part of the University of Cambridge postgraduate admissions process. Below is the complete application process. APPLY NOW Step 1: Choose Your Postgraduate Course Applicants should first identify the Cambridge postgraduate programme they wish to study. Research should include: Course content Entry requirements Duration Department Research opportunities Career outcomes Research your chosen course of study via the University postgraduate website Step 2: Identify Suitable Scholarships Applicants should search the Cambridge Trust scholarship database to identify awards matching their: Nationality Degree level Academic discipline Financial circumstances Other eligibility criteria Some partner-funded scholarships require an additional application submitted directly to the funding partner. Step 3: Choose a Cambridge College Applicants may: Select up to two preferred Colleges, or Leave the College selection blank for automatic consideration. Some scholarships are linked to particular Colleges. More information on colleges and postgraduate application can be found here. Step 4: Submit the University Application Applications must be submitted online through the University of Cambridge Applicant Portal . To be considered for Cambridge Trust funding, applicants must: Complete the funding section. Indicate they wish to be considered for funding. Submit all required supporting documents . Upload references . Pay the application fee or obtain a fee waiver . Receive a conditional offer of admission. Failure to indicate interest in funding means applicants will not be considered for Cambridge Trust scholarships. Supporting Documents Although requirements vary by programme, applicants may need: Academic transcripts Degree certificates Curriculum Vitae (CV) Personal statement Research proposal (for research programmes) English language test results Academic references Financial information (where applicable) Funding Deadlines Applicants should note an important distinction: The funding deadline is different from the admissions deadline. Every postgraduate course has its own funding deadline. Applicants should carefully check the funding deadline listed on the specific Cambridge course page before applying. Missing the funding deadline means applicants will generally not be considered for Cambridge Trust funding. If you want to be considered for funding , you can tell us in your portal application. Application Timeline The scholarship selection process generally follows this schedule: Submit postgraduate admissions application. Meet the relevant funding deadline. Receive a conditional offer of admission. Scholarship applications are reviewed. Awards are made between March and July . Applications remain under consideration throughout the awards period. Full details can be found on the University’s page Applying for postgraduate courses at Cambridge . Selection Process Cambridge Trust scholarships are highly competitive. Selection may consider: Academic excellence Research potential Financial circumstances Scholarship-specific eligibility Strength of the overall application Only successful applicants are notified. Due to the large number of applications received, unsuccessful applicants do not receive individual feedback. Why Apply for a Cambridge Trust Scholarship? Studying at Cambridge offers access to: One of the world s leading universities. Internationally recognised academics. World-class research facilities. Global professional networks. Prestigious Cambridge Colleges. Excellent career opportunities after graduation. Receiving a Cambridge Trust scholarship also enables talented students to focus on academic achievement while reducing financial pressures associated with postgraduate education. APPLY NOW Tips for a Strong Scholarship Application To improve your chances of success: Apply well before your funding deadline. Carefully research every scholarship for which you qualify. Demonstrate outstanding academic achievement. Clearly explain your financial circumstances where required. Prepare high-quality supporting documents. Secure strong academic references. Ensure every section of the funding application is completed accurately. Further help In the University’s application portal, there are additional help pages which applicants can link to directly once the application is started. These links will take you straight there: Academic history Additional information Adjustments College membership Contact details Contextual data Employment history English language Equal opportunities Nationality and visa Other information Personal details Reference request Research Important Notes Applicants should remember: You must request funding within your University application . Funding consideration is only possible if you submit your admissions application by the relevant funding deadline. Some partner scholarships require additional applications. Scholarship competition is extremely competitive. Successful applicants are notified during the March–July awards period. After you apply Once you ve submitted your application you can: Track your application and update your details as needed. Find out how we assess your application and when you’ll hear back. Review what to do after you get the outcome of your application . If you receive an offer Check how to meet the conditions of your offer Check visa requirements (if you need one) Find out how to change your start date if necessary Conclusion The Cambridge Trust Postgraduate Scholarships represent one of the most prestigious funding opportunities available to Master s and PhD students worldwide. By supporting academically outstanding individuals with financial need—and, in some cases, exceptional merit alone—the Trust enables talented scholars to pursue postgraduate education at one of the world s leading universities. Prospective applicants should begin preparing early, thoroughly research their chosen course and scholarship options, and ensure they submit a complete University of Cambridge application before the relevant funding deadline. With careful preparation and a strong academic profile, a Cambridge Trust scholarship can open the door to world-class education, cutting-edge research, and a global network of academic and professional opportunities. Application Deadline: Varies by postgraduate course. Applicants must submit their University of Cambridge admissions application by the course-specific funding deadline , which differs from the general admissions deadline. Applicants should check the funding deadline on the relevant course page before applying. For more Scholarship opportunities, please visit OFY website here . title: Memories.ai Fall Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Paid AI Research Fellowship for Master’s Students and Early-Career Researchers
summary: Artificial Intelligence is advancing at an unprecedented pace, creating exciting opportunities for emerging researchers to contribute to groundbreaking innovations. One such opportunity is the Memories.ai Fall Research Fellowship 2026 , a prestigious 12-week paid research fellowship designed for Master s students and early-career researchers passionate about computer vision, multimodal artificial intelligence, video understanding, and large-scale AI systems. Applications are now open for this highly competitive fellowship, which offers participants the chance to work alongside the research team at Memories.ai , a Silicon Valley AI company developing the world s first Large Visual Memory Model (LVMM) . Selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend, mentorship from experienced AI researchers, access to advanced GPU infrastructure, proprietary datasets, publication support, and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge AI research. With only 5 to 8 fellows selected worldwide , this fellowship represents an exceptional opportunity for aspiring AI researchers looking to build their research careers while working on real-world challenges at the forefront of artificial intelligence. About the Memories.ai Fall Research Fellowship 2026 The Memories.ai Fall Research Fellowship is a 12-week intensive research program that runs from September to November 2026 . Unlike conventional internships that primarily involve completing assigned tasks, this fellowship treats participants as genuine research collaborators. Fellows become embedded within the Memories.ai research team, working directly on open research challenges that have the potential to shape the future of visual artificial intelligence. Throughout the fellowship, participants receive: Weekly one-on-one mentorship Access to enterprise-grade computing resources Proprietary research datasets Research publication support Opportunities to collaborate with leading AI researchers Exposure to real-world research problems in visual AI The program concludes with research presentations in November 2026, allowing fellows to showcase their findings and contributions. Fellowship Overview Details Information Program Memories.ai Fall Research Fellowship 2026 Duration 12 Weeks Fellowship Period September – November 2026 Location Remote-friendly with optional in-person collaboration Number of Fellows 5–8 Monthly Stipend USD $1,000 Visa Sponsorship Available Final Presentations November 2026 About Memories.ai Memories.ai is a Silicon Valley-based artificial intelligence company focused on solving one of AI s most challenging problems: enabling machines to remember visual information over extended periods. Rather than simply recognizing objects in individual images or short videos, Memories.ai is building technology that allows AI systems to retain structured visual memories that can later be searched, queried, and analyzed. This technology forms the foundation of the company s Large Visual Memory Model (LVMM) . The research conducted at Memories.ai aims to support future AI systems capable of: Remembering what they have previously observed Understanding long-duration video content Answering questions based on accumulated visual memories Supporting autonomous AI agents operating in real-world environments What is the Large Visual Memory Model (LVMM)? The Large Visual Memory Model (LVMM) represents Memories.ai s flagship research initiative. Traditional computer vision systems typically analyze individual frames or short video clips independently. In contrast, an LVMM enables AI systems to: Process continuous video streams Build persistent memories from visual observations Retrieve past visual information on demand Understand spatial and temporal relationships Perform long-term reasoning over visual experiences This technology has the potential to transform industries including robotics, autonomous systems, security, manufacturing, augmented reality, and intelligent assistants. Research Areas Selected fellows will work across one or more of five major research themes. 1. Egocentric Video Understanding Researchers will explore AI systems that interpret the world from a first-person perspective using wearable cameras and augmented reality devices. Projects focus on: Continuous video understanding Human-centered perception Long-term temporal reasoning First-person visual experiences 2. Large Visual Memory Models (LVMM) Participants will contribute directly to Memories.ai s primary research direction. Areas include: Persistent visual memory Long-term scene understanding Retrieval systems Memory-based AI architectures Real-time visual reasoning 3. Real-Time Video Intelligence This research area focuses on processing live video streams with extremely low latency. Applications include: Smart surveillance Industrial automation Manufacturing Operations monitoring Intelligent infrastructure 4. Multimodal Memory Systems Researchers develop systems capable of integrating: Video Audio Text Cross-modal reasoning Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) These systems help AI understand multiple forms of information simultaneously. 5. Data Infrastructure at Scale Participants interested in large-scale AI infrastructure may work on: Massive video datasets Data indexing Distributed processing Retrieval systems Scalable AI pipelines Previous Research Achievements Memories.ai has already produced several notable research projects, including: SpatialMem – A spatial memory system for creating searchable 3D maps from RGB video. O-MARC – A framework that improves multimodal inference speed by over 34% while reducing memory usage. OmniRetriever – A unified retrieval model integrating audio, video, and text. MARC – A metric-aligned representation system for structured scene understanding. Fellows may have opportunities to build upon or contribute to similar groundbreaking research projects. Fellowship Benefits The Memories.ai Research Fellowship offers numerous benefits beyond financial support. Monthly Stipend Each fellow receives: USD $1,000 per month Financial support throughout the 12-week fellowship Unlimited Cloud Computing Participants gain access to: AWS cloud compute credits Google Cloud Platform (GCP) resources Large-scale AI training infrastructure Dedicated GPU Access Researchers receive access to dedicated GPU clusters for training sophisticated AI models without restrictions on computing time. Proprietary Datasets Fellows work with exclusive internal datasets that are unavailable to the wider research community. Personalized Mentorship Participants receive weekly one-on-one mentoring from senior AI researchers, including former Meta Research Scientists. Research Publication Support The fellowship actively supports publication at prestigious conferences, including: CVPR NeurIPS ICLR Other leading AI conferences Support includes: Paper preparation Submission guidance Co-authorship mentoring Research networking International Opportunities International applicants benefit from: Visa sponsorship Immigration support Remote participation options Optional in-person collaboration Who Should Apply? The fellowship is ideal for individuals who are passionate about advancing artificial intelligence research. Eligible applicants include: Master s students in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or related fields. Recent Bachelor s degree graduates pursuing research careers. Early-career AI researchers. Individuals with experience using PyTorch, JAX, or equivalent deep learning frameworks. Applicants with research publications or active research projects. Researchers interested in computer vision, multimodal AI, video understanding, egocentric vision, or data infrastructure. Importantly, Memories.ai emphasizes that applicants are evaluated based on research ability and intellectual curiosity rather than institutional prestige , encouraging talented researchers from all institutions to apply. Why This Fellowship Stands Out The Memories.ai Fall Research Fellowship distinguishes itself by offering: Direct collaboration with an industry-leading AI research team. Real research ownership rather than routine internship tasks. Access to world-class computing resources. Opportunities to publish research in top-tier conferences. Personalized mentorship from experienced researchers. A fully remote-friendly structure with optional in-person collaboration. International accessibility through visa sponsorship. These features make it an outstanding opportunity for aspiring AI researchers seeking meaningful research experience. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official Memories.ai application portal. Applicants with questions about the fellowship may contact: Email: Lily.x@memories.ai The company encourages Master s students and early-career researchers interested in visual AI to apply. APPLY NOW Application Deadline At the time of publication, no specific application deadline has been announced . Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible, as only 5–8 fellows will be selected for the Fall 2026 cohort. Conclusion The Memories.ai Fall Research Fellowship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for aspiring AI researchers to engage in pioneering work on computer vision, multimodal AI, and Large Visual Memory Models. With generous research support, access to advanced infrastructure, personalized mentorship, publication opportunities, and a monthly stipend, the fellowship provides an ideal environment for developing both technical expertise and academic research credentials. For Master s students and early-career researchers eager to contribute to the next generation of artificial intelligence, this fellowship represents a valuable stepping stone toward a successful research career. Prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early and take advantage of this unique opportunity to work alongside one of Silicon Valley s innovative AI research teams. For more Fellowship opportunities, visit the OFY website here . title: The American Council of Learned Societies (ACLS) 2026–27 Fellowships and Grants
summary: About the American Council of Learned Societies The American Council of Learned Societies is a nonprofit federation representing scholarly organizations in the humanities and social sciences. It works with scholars, universities, learned societies, foundations and other institutions to strengthen research, scholarly communication and the broader infrastructure supporting knowledge production. ACLS supports research in fields such as history, philosophy, religious studies, literature, linguistics, anthropology, archaeology, sociology, political science, economics, gender studies, ethnic studies, legal studies, geography, art history, architectural history, film and media studies, musicology, psychology, science and technology studies, communication, theatre and performance studies. Research in the social sciences is generally expected to use humanistic, qualitative or interpretive approaches. Interdisciplinary and cross-disciplinary projects are also welcomed across many ACLS programs. In addition to financial assistance, ACLS programs may connect award recipients with: Mentoring and professional-development opportunities Scholarly networks and peer communities Workshops and cohort meetings Career-development activities Public-engagement initiatives Opportunities to exchange knowledge with researchers from other institutions Support for communicating research beyond traditional academic audiences Awardees are selected through peer-review committees composed of scholars appointed to evaluate applications under the relevant program criteria. Important Information About the 2026–27 ACLS Competition Cycle Application deadlines differ by competition. However, ACLS has confirmed that every competition in the 2026–27 cycle will close at 5:00 PM Eastern Time on its respective deadline. Applicants should not assume that a deadline extends until midnight. Applications must be fully completed, uploaded and submitted before 5:00 PM ET. Saving an incomplete application in the online system does not constitute submission. ACLS also states that its application, peer-review and award-management processes are intended to promote inclusive excellence and broaden access to research support across the academic humanities and higher-education sector. Complete ACLS 2026–27 Application Deadline Calendar September 17, 2026 ACLS Fellowships October 28, 2026 Luce/ACLS Dissertation Fellowships in American Art Mellon/ACLS Dissertation Innovation Fellowships Summer Institute for the Study of East Central and Southeastern Europe The Robert H. N. Ho Family Foundation Dissertation Fellowships in Buddhist Studies The Robert H. N. Ho Family Foundation Early Career Research Fellowships in Buddhist Studies November 5, 2026 Luce/ACLS Early Career Fellowships in China Studies – Long-Term Luce/ACLS Early Career Fellowships in China Studies – Flexible Luce/ACLS Travel Grants in China Studies November 11, 2026 ACLS HBCU Faculty Fellowships ACLS Project Grants for HBCU Faculty November 20, 2026 ACLS Digital Justice Development Grants ACLS Digital Justice Seed Grants The Robert H. N. Ho Family Foundation Public Impact Grants in Buddhist Studies January 13, 2027 ACLS Graduate Internships on the Spiritual Infrastructure of the Future February 1, 2027 The Robert H. N. Ho Family Foundation New Professorships in Buddhist Studies March 10, 2027 ACLS Leading Edge Fellowships April 2, 2027 The Robert H. N. Ho Family Foundation Buddhism Public Scholars All the deadlines listed above are scheduled to close at 5:00 PM ET. ACLS Fellowships Application deadline: September 17, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The ACLS Fellowship is one of the organization’s principal research-support programs. It is designed to provide scholars with sustained time to undertake research and writing in the humanities and interpretive social sciences. The program supports projects from different disciplines, historical periods, regions and methodological traditions. Proposed outputs may include: Scholarly monographs Academic articles Critical editions Digital research projects Publicly engaged humanities projects Research resources Other substantial scholarly works The latest published program information identifies a maximum award of US$60,000 , with support generally structured around six to twelve months of full-time research or writing. Shorter award periods may receive proportionately adjusted funding. Applicants should carefully review the updated 2026–27 competition page because award periods, documentation requirements and other administrative details can change between cycles. Luce/ACLS Dissertation Fellowships in American Art Application deadline: October 28, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The Luce/ACLS Dissertation Fellowships in American Art support advanced doctoral research relating to the history of the visual arts of the United States. The program has operated through a long-standing partnership between ACLS and the Henry Luce Foundation. It contributes to the development of emerging scholars working on American art, including research that broadens the geographic, cultural, methodological and historical boundaries of the field. Projects may examine subjects such as: Painting, sculpture and visual culture Architecture and the built environment Photography Indigenous and Native American art Decorative arts Material culture Digital and media-based art Artistic communities and institutions Transnational dimensions of American art Questions of identity, representation and cultural exchange The fellowship is intended to help recipients complete significant dissertation research and writing while developing their contribution to the field of art history. Mellon/ACLS Dissertation Innovation Fellowships Application deadline: October 28, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The Mellon/ACLS Dissertation Innovation Fellowship supports doctoral researchers developing ambitious and methodologically innovative dissertation projects in the humanities and interpretive social sciences. The program intervenes during the formative stages of dissertation development, before the research and writing process is substantially advanced. It encourages scholars to reconsider conventional assumptions about what a dissertation can examine, how research can be conducted and what formats scholarly work can take. Proposals may involve: New or experimental research methods Interdisciplinary approaches Digital or multimedia scholarship Community-engaged research Work with unconventional archives or sources Collaborative knowledge production Research involving underrepresented communities Alternative formats for presenting scholarly findings Published program information describes a US$42,000 base stipend , additional project-related funding of up to US$8,000 , and separate support for external mentorship. Applicants must rely on the updated 2026–27 guidelines for the final award structure and eligible expenditure rules. Summer Institute for the Study of East Central and Southeastern Europe Application deadline: October 28, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The Summer Institute for the Study of East Central and Southeastern Europe provides an intensive environment for research, writing, fieldwork and interdisciplinary scholarly exchange. The initiative is organized by ACLS in partnership with the Centre for Advanced Study Sofia. Previous institutes have brought together researchers from Eastern Europe and North America for a focused residency in Bulgaria. The institute is intended to encourage: Concentrated research and writing Local or regional fieldwork Cross-disciplinary discussion International scholarly collaboration Development of new research networks Exchange between researchers working inside and outside the region With renewed support, the initiative is continuing through 2026 and 2027. Robert H. N. Ho Family Foundation Dissertation Fellowships in Buddhist Studies Application deadline: October 28, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The Dissertation Fellowships in Buddhist Studies form part of the Robert H. N. Ho Family Foundation Program in Buddhist Studies administered by ACLS. The program supports the academic study of Buddhism across different regions, historical periods, languages, intellectual traditions and methodological approaches. Research may address areas such as: Buddhist philosophy and intellectual history Buddhist texts and translation Religious practice and ritual Buddhist art and architecture Monastic institutions Buddhism and political life Contemporary Buddhist communities Buddhism, migration and globalisation Digital approaches to Buddhist Studies Interreligious and cross-cultural interaction The dissertation fellowships are intended to help researchers make substantial progress toward completing original doctoral work in Buddhist Studies. Robert H. N. Ho Family Foundation Early Career Research Fellowships in Buddhist Studies Application deadline: October 28, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The Early Career Research Fellowships support emerging researchers undertaking significant new work relating to Buddhist traditions. The fellowships can provide time for research, writing, translation and the development of scholarly outputs. All topics, periods and methodological approaches within Buddhist Studies may be considered, subject to the detailed requirements of the competition. The wider Ho Family Foundation program also helps strengthen international scholarly networks, improve the visibility of new Buddhist Studies research and connect scholars with communities of practice and broader public audiences. Luce/ACLS Early Career Fellowships in China Studies Application deadline: November 5, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET Two Early Career Fellowship options will be available under the Luce/ACLS Program in China Studies: Long-Term Early Career Fellowship Flexible Early Career Fellowship Both programs seek to promote high-quality research on China, Chinese cultures, histories, societies and communities. They also aim to strengthen scholarly networks and contribute to informed public understanding of China. Long-Term Fellowship The long-term option is intended for research and writing projects requiring a sustained period of concentrated work. This may include archival research, fieldwork, data collection, manuscript preparation or the completion of a major scholarly output. Flexible Fellowship The flexible option is designed for projects that can be undertaken through shorter periods of work distributed across a broader timeframe. This structure may be useful where continuous leave from professional responsibilities is not practical. Applicants should compare the two options carefully and select the structure that corresponds to their research plan, institutional responsibilities and proposed outputs. Luce/ACLS Travel Grants in China Studies Application deadline: November 5, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The Luce/ACLS Travel Grants in China Studies provide targeted assistance for research-related travel. Depending on the updated guidelines, relevant activities may include: Archival research Fieldwork Access to libraries and collections Research interviews Participation in scholarly meetings Development of research networks Travel necessary to advance a China Studies project Applicants should provide a clear connection between the proposed travel, the research objectives and the expected scholarly outcome. ACLS HBCU Faculty Fellowships Application deadline: November 11, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The ACLS HBCU Faculty Fellowships provide substantial support for faculty research at Historically Black Colleges and Universities. The fellowships are designed for projects requiring sustained research engagement. Funding may be used to create meaningful time and conditions for scholarship through: Semester-long or year-long research leave Summer research salary Course releases Archival or field research Conference participation Research and editorial assistance Project-related collaboration Publication and dissemination costs Childcare or eldercare expenses that enable research engagement Published information describes fellowship awards ranging from US$30,000 to US$50,000 , depending on the proposed budget and research plan. The objective is not simply to fund isolated expenses, but to enable substantial advancement of a research-grounded project. Potential outcomes may include academic publications, public humanities projects, community-engaged scholarship, pedagogical resources, digital projects or creative work grounded in scholarly research. ACLS Project Grants for HBCU Faculty Application deadline: November 11, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The ACLS Project Grants for HBCU Faculty support early-stage project design and smaller-scale research development. Unlike the longer fellowship model, these grants are particularly suitable for exploratory work or a defined stage of a broader project. Funding may help recipients: Conduct preliminary research Test a research method Visit an archive or collection Organize a research workshop Develop community partnerships Hire research or editorial support Prepare a project for future external funding Advance an existing project to its next stage The latest published program description provides grants of up to US$10,000 . Applicants should choose between the HBCU fellowship and project grant according to the scale, maturity, timeframe and financial requirements of the proposed work. ACLS Digital Justice Development Grants Application deadline: November 20, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The ACLS Digital Justice Development Grants support established digital humanities projects seeking to deepen their intellectual, technical, institutional or community impact. The Digital Justice program is intended to advance digital scholarship while addressing structural inequalities affecting access, representation, participation and control within digital knowledge systems. Development-stage projects may seek support to: Expand an existing digital resource Strengthen technical infrastructure Improve accessibility Build or deepen community partnerships Address gaps in digital collections Develop sustainable project governance Improve the preservation of digital materials Support responsible and ethical data practices Increase the public accessibility of research Applicants should demonstrate how their project advances both scholarly value and principles of digital justice. ACLS Digital Justice Seed Grants Application deadline: November 20, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET The Digital Justice Seed Grants are intended for projects at an earlier stage of planning or development. Seed support can help teams move from an initial idea toward a viable digital humanities initiative by funding activities such as: Project planning Community consultation Prototype development Initial collection or digitisation Partnership building Technical assessment Accessibility planning Governance and sustainability design Preliminary research Testing new digital methods A competitive proposal should explain the problem being addressed, the people or communities involved, the proposed digital approach and how the seed-funded work will prepare the project for responsible long-term development. Possible outputs may include: Exhibitions Public lectures Educational resources Digital platforms Documentary or multimedia projects Community programs Translations and accessible publications Workshops and public discussions Projects should combine credible scholarship with a clearly defined public purpose, audience and communication strategy. Internship placements may allow participants to apply research, analysis, writing and communication skills to real organizational challenges while gaining experience in possible careers beyond the university. The program is particularly relevant to research concerning: Religion and public life Community institutions Civic participation Social trust Religious diversity Spiritual care Culture and collective identity The role of faith-based organizations in society The precise placement structure and participating organizations should be confirmed through the updated competition materials. Ho Family Foundation Buddhism Public Scholars How to Prepare a Competitive ACLS Application Although every competition has its own requirements, applicants can improve their preparation by following a structured process. 1. Select the correct program Applicants should not select a competition simply because it provides a larger award. The program must match the project’s: Career stage Academic subject Research methodology Geographic or thematic focus Institutional setting Expected outcome Required timeframe Funding needs 2. Read the current competition page and FAQ Requirements can change from one year to the next. Previous application samples are useful for understanding structure, but they should not override the current 2026–27 guidelines. Each applicant should verify: Eligibility requirements Award amount Eligible costs Award period Required documents Formatting instructions Reference requirements Institutional endorsements Submission procedure 3. Obtain an ORCID iD where required ACLS states that many of its programs require applicants to have an ORCID iD. Applicants who do not already have one should arrange it before beginning the final submission process. 4. Define a clear research problem A strong proposal should explain: What the project investigates Why the issue is important What gap exists in current knowledge How the research will address that gap Which sources or evidence will be used What methods will be applied What outcome will be produced Why the applicant is positioned to carry out the work 5. Develop a realistic work plan The proposal and work plan must be consistent. Applicants should divide the project into achievable stages and explain when major activities will occur. A work plan may include: Preliminary research Archival or field research Data or source analysis Writing Revision External consultation Digital development Public engagement Publication preparation Dissemination 6. Prepare a justified budget Where a budget is required, every major expense should be linked to a project activity or outcome. Applicants should avoid: Unexplained lump-sum figures Costs not permitted under the program Expenses that do not relate to the work plan Excessive travel without a clear research purpose Unrealistic or unsupported estimates 7. Write for an interdisciplinary review committee Peer reviewers may not be specialists in the applicant’s precise subfield. Proposals should therefore be academically rigorous without becoming inaccessible. Specialized terminology should be explained, and the significance of the project should be understandable to scholars from related disciplines. 8. Submit before the final day All 2026–27 competitions close at 5:00 PM ET. Applicants should allow sufficient time for: Uploading documents Checking file formats Confirming references Obtaining institutional approvals Reviewing online form entries Resolving technical issues Completing the final submission Submitting several days before the deadline is safer than attempting to complete the application during the final hours. Can Applicants Apply for More Than One ACLS Program? ACLS states that individuals may apply to multiple programs during the same competition year as long as they independently satisfy the requirements of each competition. However, an individual may accept no more than one ACLS fellowship within a single competition year. Applicants should therefore ensure that every application is genuinely aligned with the relevant program rather than submitting the same proposal indiscriminately across different competitions. Conclusion The ACLS 2026–27 fellowship and grant competitions provide a substantial collection of opportunities for advancing humanities and social science research, doctoral development, digital scholarship, public engagement and institutional capacity. With deadlines extending from September 17, 2026, to April 2, 2027, prospective applicants have different preparation periods depending on the program. The earliest deadline is for the ACLS Fellowships, while later competitions include institutional Buddhist Studies support, nonprofit career placements and public scholarship opportunities. Applicants should begin by identifying the competition that most closely matches their project and professional stage. They should then review the updated guidelines, prepare a focused proposal, develop a realistic work plan and budget, and complete all required documents well before the 5:00 PM ET submission deadline. The breadth of the 2026–27 competitions reflects ACLS’s continuing commitment to supporting rigorous scholarship, strengthening research communities and expanding the contribution of humanities and social science knowledge to institutions and the wider public.
